claims.claim: Increasing the solution-phase volume fraction of the negative electrode (εs,n) from 0.3 to 0.7 shifts the discharge curve from right to left and decreases battery capacity.
claims.quote: the increase in the solution-phase volume fraction shifts the discharge curve from right to left and decreases the battery capacity.

claims.claim: The electronic conductivity of the positive and negative electrodes has a significant effect on the IR drop only at high current densities.
claims.quote: The electronic conductivity of the positive and negative electrodes each has a significant effect on the IR drop only at high current densities.

claims.claim: Using the heuristic algorithm with a divide-and-conquer strategy, the complete identification of P2D model parameters can be performed within 10 hours using a single core computer.
claims.quote: With the heuristic algorithm, the full set of P2D parameters can be performed within 10 h using a single core computer.

claims.claim: A prior method using a genetic algorithm required three weeks to complete identification of 88 parameters using a cluster of five quad-core computers.
claims.quote: It took three weeks to complete an 88 parameters identiﬁcation using a cluster of ﬁve quad-core computers and a genetic algorithm.

claims.claim: The percentile error of simulated results with identified parameters was 78.1 mV or less, and the average error was approximately 12.9 mV or less across discharge rates of 0.1C, 0.5C, 1C, 2C, and 3C.
claims.quote: The percentile error of the simulated results with the identiﬁed parameters is 78.1 mV or less, and the average error of the simulated results is about 12.9 mV or less.

claims.claim: Isolated solid electrolytic salts (Cases 7 and 8 in Table III) exhibit high activation energy values that prevent the initiation of a thermal runaway, whereas experiments involving organic solvents show significantly smaller EA values, implying that organic solvents act as promoters of the runaway event.
claims.quote: comparing the values obtained from the different DSC proﬁles as published by different authors for solid samples of electrolytes, it can be derived that the high EA values evidenced in the presence of isolated solid electrolytic salts (Cases 7 and 8 in Table III) prevent the initiation of a thermal runaway. Experiments involving organic solvents on the other hand, where EA values for the reaction global rate were signiﬁcantly smaller, could imply that organic solvents act as promoters of the runaway event.

claims.claim: LiPF6 could act as the limiting reactant which, via its decomposition, controls the series of runaway reactions, independent of the cathodic material present.
claims.quote: it can be concluded that independent of the cathodic material, LiPF6 could act as the limiting reactant which via its decomposition controls the series of runaway reactions.

claims.claim: The total loss of cooling capacity and overheating above temperatures of design and control will drive a thermally autocatalytic runaway of a series of reactions, representing the worst-case scenario for the battery.
claims.quote: the total loss of cooling capacity and overheating above temperatures of design and control, which will drive to a thermally autocatalytic runaway of a series of reactions, is the worst-case scenario for the battery

claims.claim: In Criticality Class V, values of pressure and temperature reach maximum values inside the cell, cooling capacity as a barrier is null, and venting with flammable and toxic gas release increases with time until gas ignition and pressure release by explosion.
claims.quote: Loss of control with respect to the expected normal operation temperature. Values of pressure and temperature reach maximum values inside the cell. Cooling capacity as barrier is null at this point. Venting event, ﬂammable and toxic gas release increases with time. Critical point is reached when gas catches ﬁre and pressure is release by explosion event.

claims.claim: The calculated orders of reaction n for LiPF6 across different data sets were found to lie between 0.57 and 1.38, making it reasonable to treat the decomposition as first order.
claims.quote: The calculated orders of reaction, n, for LiPF6 in different data set results were found to lie between 0.57 and 1.38; thus it is reasonable to treat the decomposition as ﬁrst order.

claims.claim: For NMC cathodes, reducing carbon black content from 5.88 to 4.00 wt% and CB:B ratio to CB:B=1 leads to significantly increased specific electronic resistances across all mass loading configurations.
claims.quote: The speciﬁc electronic resistances of NMC cathodes measured in this study conﬁrm this ﬁnding as they are signiﬁcantly lower at high carbon black (CB) contents and CB:B > 1 for all mass loading (ML) conﬁgurations

claims.claim: For NMC cathodes with high mass loading, specific discharge capacity approaches 0 mAh/g for non-calendered cathodes at 5C, whereas low mass loading cathodes show only about 50% deterioration compared to the high calendering configuration.
claims.quote: At low mass loadings the deterioration of speciﬁc discharge capacity for medium and low calendering conﬁgurations is about 50% compared to the conﬁguration with high calendering stress load. However, this deterioration is still relatively small compared to the cells with high mass loadings where speciﬁc discharge capacity even approaches 0 mAh/g for non calendered cathodes.

claims.claim: Ionic conductivity limitations are expected to become significant only at calendering stress loads beyond 22% for NMC and 30% for LMO, within the manufacturing parameters studied.
claims.quote: Limits of ion transport which are expected to occur in electrodes which are calendered too strongly, appear to become only signiﬁcant at calendering stress load beyond 22% for NMC and 30% for LMO.

claims.claim: Increasing calendering stress load reduces standard deviations of discharge capacities among identically produced cells, with the largest improvement occurring between the non-calendered and medium calendering level.
claims.quote: By far the highest standard deviations of discharge capacities were observed among cells with non calendered cathodes. The deviations decrease with the increase of calendering stress load. The largest improvement occurs between non calendering and the medium calendering level.

claims.claim: NMC cathodes show higher specific discharge capacities at low C-rates, while LMO cathodes show higher specific discharge capacities at high C-rates, with the crossover C-rate shifting toward higher values as calendering stress load increases.
claims.quote: NMC has higher speciﬁc discharge capacities at low C-rates and LMO shows higher speciﬁc discharge capacities at higher C-rates... the C-rate above which LMO becomes superior to NMC can be shifted toward higher C-rates by increasing the calendering stress load.
